title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Weekend Recap - July 19, 2026. Data based on Friday, July 17, 2026 market close.

items.headline: Tech Sell-Off and Geopolitical Tensions Drive Indices Lower on Friday
items.summary: U.S. major indices concluded the trading week on a negative note, as a significant sell-off in semiconductor stocks, escalating Middle East tensions driving oil prices higher, and a disappointing earnings report from Netflix weighed heavily on market sentiment. The S&P 500, NASDAQ, and Dow all registered declines, reflecting a broader pivot out of the AI trade and increased risk aversion.

items.headline: Travelers Companies Inc Leads Financial Gainers
items.summary: The Travelers Companies Inc saw a significant jump in its stock price, contributing to the financial sector's strong performance this quarter.

items.headline: Seagate Technology Holdings Rises Amidst Tech Volatility
items.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC posted notable gains despite a broader tech sell-off, indicating some resilient areas within the sector.

items.headline: Centene Corp Sees Strong Advance
items.summary: Centene Corp. experienced a solid uptick in its share value during Friday's trading session.

items.headline: Netflix Plunges Post-Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Netflix fell more than 10% following its quarterly earnings report, as revenue narrowly missed consensus and its Q3 forecast disappointed investors.

items.headline: Semiconductor Stocks Witness Steep Declines
items.summary: The PHLX Semiconductor Index (SOX) was on track for 20% declines from recent highs, with major chip companies like ARM, AMD, Oracle, Broadcom, Super Micro Computer, and Marvell Technology seeing losses of 5% or worse.

items.headline: Vivakor Inc. Leads Individual Stock Declines
items.summary: Vivakor, Inc. recorded a substantial loss, making it one of the biggest individual stock losers on Friday.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads as Tech Lags
items.summary: On the most recent trading day, the Energy sector emerged as the best-performing sector, advancing by +1.16%, primarily driven by surging oil prices amid Middle East tensions. Real Estate also saw positive movement, up +0.08%. In contrast, the Communication Services sector lagged significantly with a -2.09% decline, alongside Consumer Discretionary (-1.44%) and Information Technology (-1.07%), reflecting a broader market rotation out of high-growth tech names.

items.headline: Fed Maintains Rates Amid Cooling Inflation, Future Hikes Unlikely for July
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) has kept the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50–3.75% since December 2025. Recent cooler-than-expected inflation data for June, including a 0.4% month-over-month drop in CPI, has significantly reduced the market-implied probability of a July rate hike to about 14%. Morgan Stanley Research anticipates the Fed will remain on hold, with the next FOMC meeting scheduled for July 29th. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has indicated a preference for less forward guidance, increasing market sensitivity to economic data.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season in Full Swing with Key Tech Reports Ahead
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is in its third-busiest week, with 86 S&P 500 companies reporting. A robust 88% of S&P 500 firms are exceeding earnings estimates, contributing to a blended earnings growth rate of 24.7% year-over-year. The 'Magnificent 7' tech giants are projected to see a 31.1% earnings surge. All eyes are on Tesla ($TSLA) and Alphabet ($GOOGL), which are set to report earnings the week of July 21st.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices, Market Volatility
items.summary: Escalating tensions in the Middle East have pushed oil prices higher, notably with WTI crude rising over 2% to above $80 per barrel. This geopolitical backdrop has contributed to market volatility and amplified concerns, further influencing sector rotations and overall market sentiment.
